plan_metadata.name: Sample Intelligent Data Chart Local Debug

steps.description: Type text 'intelligent data chart' into the search input field in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface within Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Create" button next to "Intelligent Data Chart Generator" in the Featured samples list within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:intelligent-data-chart-generator}} into the run time variable.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
env_file="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/intelligent-data-chart-generator/env/.env.local.user"

declare -A values=(
  [SECRET_OPENAI_ENDPOINT]="https://atk-test-open-ai.openai.azure.com/"
  [SECRET_OPENAI_API_KEY]="${{secret:AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY:}}"
  [SECRET_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME]="gpt-4.1"
  [SQL_USER]="vscuse-admin"
  [SECRET_SQL_PASSWORD]="${{secret:SECRET_SQL_PASSWORD:}}"
  [SQL_SERVER]="vscuse-testing.database.windows.net"
  [SQL_DATABASE]="intelligent-data-chart"
)

if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
  echo "File $env_file not found."
  exit 1
fi

# Backup the original env file
cp "$env_file" "$env_file.bak"

for key in "${!values[@]}"; do
  value="${values[$key]}"
  
  # If the key exists, replace value; else, add to end
  if grep -q "^${key}=" "$env_file"; then
    sed -i "s|^${key}=.*|${key}=\"${value}\"|" "$env_file"
  else
    echo "${key}=\"${value}\"" >> "$env_file"
  fi

done

echo "Updated $env_file with provided values."
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
Updated /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/intelligent-data-chart-generator/env/.env.local.user with provided values.

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description:
@code in file: "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/env/.env.local.user"
find and fill in value: 
SECRET_OPENAI_ENDPOINT="${{env:AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT}}/"
SECRET_OPENAI_API_KEY="${{secret:AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY}}"
SECRET_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME="gpt-4.1"
SQL_USER="vscuse-admin"
SECRET_SQL_PASSWORD="${{secret:SECRET_SQL_PASSWORD}}"
SQL_SERVER="vscuse-testing.database.windows.net"
SQL_DATABASE="intelligent-data-chart"
Click the center area of the Visual Studio Code workspace, where the VS Code logo is displayed and no specific UI element is present, indicating interaction with the main empty editor space.
Press **Ctrl+Shift+P** to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, while in the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" workspace.
Type the text 'debug: select and start' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Press the Enter key to select the highlighted "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type text 'debug (Chrome)' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top center of the interface.
Press the Enter key to confirm selection of "Debug (Chrome)" in the command palette of Visual Studio Code.
Click the close ("X") button on the "teams.microsoft.com wants to look for and connect to any device on your local network" permission dialog in a Microsoft Teams web app window.
Type 'https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab' into the Chrome browser's address bar on a new tab page.
Press Enter in the Chrome address bar to navigate to "https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab" on a new tab page.
Click the "Advanced" button on the Chrome privacy error page ("Your connection is not private") to reveal additional security options for proceeding to the localhost site.
Click the "Proceed to localhost (unsafe)" link on the Chrome "Your connection is not private" warning page to bypass the security warning and continue to the localhost site.

Click the "Monthly sales data of product 969" button in the Intelligent Data Chart Generator web application, located below the introductory text and to the right of the "Top 20 selling products" button.
Click the "Open" button in the Microsoft Teams web interface popup that confirms the "intelligent-chartlocal" app was added successfully.
Click the "Allow" button in the browser pop-up from teams.microsoft.com requesting permission to look for and connect to devices on your local network in Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Top 20 selling products" button in the Intelligent Data Chart Generator web interface within Microsoft Teams.
Click the "Send" button (paper plane icon) at the end of the input box in the "Intelligent Data Chart Generator" web app to submit the description "Top 20 selling products".
Press the Enter key in the text input box containing "Top 20 selling products" within the Intelligent Data Chart Generator app interface in Microsoft Teams to generate an AI-powered chart.
@assertion data chart exist.
Click the "Monthly sales data of product 969" button in the Intelligent Data Chart Generator interface within Microsoft Teams, as indicated by a red crosshair marking the interaction point.
Click the "Send" button (paper plane icon) at the end of the input box labeled "Monthly sales data of product 969" in the Intelligent Data Chart Generator panel of Microsoft Teams web app.

Press the Enter key while focused on the "Monthly sales data of product 969" text input box within the Intelligent Data Chart Generator page of Microsoft Teams (web app), to submit the query and generate AI-based charts.
@assertion data chart exist.

Click the "Sales statistics by month" button in the query options bar on the "Intelligent Data Chart Generator" page within Microsoft Teams (web app) to view monthly sales data charts. The button is visually highlighted with a gray background and located to the right of other filter options above the chart results.

Click the "Send" button (right-facing arrow icon) next to the input box labeled "Sales statistic by month" in the Intelligent Data Chart Generator app within Microsoft Teams. This action submits the query for sales statistics by month.
Press the Enter key in the “Sales statistic by month” input box of the Intelligent Data Chart Generator interface within Microsoft Teams to generate AI-powered chart results.
@assertion data chart exist.
